content.thinking:
Now I need to:
1. Generate CA, server, and client certificates using the openssl-toolbox pod
2. Create a Kubernetes secret with these certificates
3. Update the StatefulSet to mount the secret at /etc/tls
4. Reconfigure MongoDB to require TLS

Let me first check what tools are available in the openssl-toolbox pod.

content.text: Good. The replica set has 3 members (1 primary, 2 secondaries). Now I'll generate the certificates using the openssl-toolbox pod.

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- openssl version 2>&1

kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- bash -c '
set -e
mkdir -p /tmp/tls
cd /tmp/tls

# Generate CA key and certificate
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 4096
openssl req -new -x509 -days 3650 -key ca.key -out ca.crt \
  -subj "/CN=MongoDB-CA/O=MongoDB"

# Generate server key and CSR
openssl genrsa -out server.key 2048
openssl req -new -key server.key -out server.csr \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-rs/O=MongoDB"

# SAN for all replica set members + the headless service
cat > server-san.cnf <<EOF
[req]
req_extensions = v3_req
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name

[req_distinguished_name]

[v3_req]
subjectAltName = @alt_names

[alt_names]
DNS.1 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.2 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.3 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.4 = mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = localhost
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
EOF

openssl x509 -req -days 3650 \
  -in server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out server.crt \
  -extensions v3_req -extfile server-san.cnf

# MongoDB expects PEM = cert + key concatenated
cat server.crt server.key > server.pem

# Generate client key and cert
openssl genrsa -out client.key 2048
openssl req -new -key client.key -out client.csr \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-client/O=MongoDB"
openssl x509 -req -days 3650 \
  -in client.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out client.crt
cat client.crt client.key > client.pem

echo "--- Generated files ---"
ls -lh /tmp/tls/
echo "--- CA cert ---"
openssl x509 -noout -subject -issuer -in ca.crt
echo "--- Server cert ---"
openssl x509 -noout -subject -issuer -text -in server.crt | grep -E "(Subject:|Issuer:|DNS:|IP)"
echo "--- Client cert ---"
openssl x509 -noout -subject -issuer -in client.crt
' 2>&1
input.description: Generate CA, server, and client certificates in openssl-toolbox

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
set -e
mkdir -p /tmp/tls
cd /tmp/tls

# Generate CA key and certificate
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 4096
openssl req -new -x509 -days 3650 -key ca.key -out ca.crt \
  -subj "/CN=MongoDB-CA/O=MongoDB"

# Generate server key and CSR
openssl genrsa -out server.key 2048
openssl req -new -key server.key -out server.csr \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-rs/O=MongoDB"

# SAN config for all replica set members
cat > server-san.cnf <<EOF
[req]
req_extensions = v3_req
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
[req_distinguished_name]
[v3_req]
subjectAltName = @alt_names
[alt_names]
DNS.1 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.2 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.3 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.4 = mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = localhost
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
EOF

openssl x509 -req -days 3650 \
  -in server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out server.crt \
  -extensions v3_req -extfile server-san.cnf

# MongoDB PEM = cert + key
cat server.crt server.key > server.pem

# Generate client key and cert
openssl genrsa -out client.key 2048
openssl req -new -key client.key -out client.csr \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-client/O=MongoDB"
openssl x509 -req -days 3650 \
  -in client.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out client.crt
cat client.crt client.key > client.pem

echo "Done"
ls -lh /tmp/tls/
' 2>&1

content.thinking:
Certificates generated. Now I need to:
1. Copy them out of the pod to create a Kubernetes secret
2. Create a Kubernetes secret with the certs
3. Update the StatefulSet to mount the secret at /etc/tls
4. Restart the MongoDB pods with TLS flags

Let me extract the certificate contents from the pod.

content.text: Certificates generated. Now I'll extract them and create a Kubernetes secret.

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/tls/ca.crt > /tmp/ca.crt
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/tls/server.pem > /tmp/server.pem
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/tls/client.pem > /tmp/client.pem
echo "Extracted. Sizes:"
wc -c /tmp/ca.crt /tmp/server.pem /tmp/client.pem
input.description: Copy certificates from openssl-toolbox to local /tmp

input.command:
kubectl create secret generic mongo-tls \
  --from-file=ca.crt=/tmp/ca.crt \
  --from-file=server.pem=/tmp/server.pem \
  --from-file=client.pem=/tmp/client.pem \
  -n mongodb 2>&1
input.description: Create mongo-tls secret in mongodb namespace

content.text: Now I'll patch the StatefulSet to mount the secret at `/etc/tls` and add TLS flags to `mongod`.

input.description: Patch StatefulSet to add TLS flags and mount mongo-tls secret

input.command:
# Wait for rolling update to complete (pods restart in order: 2, 1, 0)
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  READY=$(kubectl get statefulset mongo-rs -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null)
  UPDATED=$(kubectl get statefulset mongo-rs -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.status.updatedReplicas}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) ready=$READY updated=$UPDATED"
  if [ "$READY" = "3" ] && [ "$UPDATED" = "3" ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods updated and ready"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Wait for rolling update of mongo-rs to complete

description: Wait for rolling update of mongo-rs to complete
